data.title: Episodic Memory Agent
data.name: Episodic Memory Agent
data.subtitle: A Sensor-Agnostic Cognitive Architecture for Event-Segmented Memory Formation
data.summary: An event-segmented episodic memory agent designed to simulate how an intelligent system perceives, interprets, and organizes experience over time. The architecture centers on a strict cognitive loop—resolving location, identifying entities, detecting change—and freezing those moments into structured episodes. Built with swappable modules, strict invariants, and full logging, the system prioritizes learning over hardcoded assumptions, user-defined semantics over predefined categories, and reproducible behavior over opaque heuristics.
data.oneLiner: A modular cognitive agent framework that models how experience becomes memory.

articles.title: Consolidating an Offline-First Episodic Memory System

readme.content:
# Episodic Memory

This repository is now organized around one canonical Python package, `episodic_memory`, and one canonical web app in `dashboard/`.

The merged project is offline-first and built around a single artifact bundle that notebooks, the inspector API, and the Next.js dashboard all read.

## What It Does

- runs deterministic stub scenarios against the canonical working-memory core
- runs panorama image or video exploration for location identification
- records one canonical run bundle for every execution
- exposes read-only inspection through Jupyter notebooks and the dashboard
- imports legacy `episodic-memory-agent` run directories into the canonical bundle format

## Canonical Layout

- `src/episodic_memory/`: canonical Python package
- `dashboard/`: canonical Next.js dashboard
- `notebooks/`: canonical notebook workspace
- `docs/`: architecture, runtime, validation, migration, and notebook docs
- `archive/`: archived legacy codebases after migration

## Install

```bash
pip install -e .[dev,notebook]
```

## Run

```bash
# Stub scenario
episodic-memory run --mode stub --scenario scenario_e

# Panorama location-identification flow
episodic-memory run --mode panorama --image-dir tests/fixtures/panorama

# Import an old episodic-memory-agent run
episodic-memory import-legacy archive/episodic-memory-agent/runs/<run_dir>

# Start the read-only inspector API
episodic-memory inspect --run-root runs
```

## Dashboard

```bash
cd dashboard
npm install
npm run dev
```

The dashboard expects the inspector API at `http://127.0.0.1:8780` by default. Override with `INSPECTOR_API_BASE` if needed.

## Notebook Workspace

```bash
jupyter lab
```

Canonical notebooks:

- `notebooks/01_pipeline_workspace.ipynb`
- `notebooks/02_memory_explorer.ipynb`
- `notebooks/03_run_comparison_and_dashboard_parity.ipynb`

## Canonical Artifact Bundle

Every run writes:

- `manifest.json`
- `summary.json`
- `memories.json`
- `graph.json`
- `events.jsonl`
- `memory_decisions.jsonl`
- `steps/step_XXXX.snapshot.json`

This is the shared contract used by notebooks, the inspector API, and the dashboard.

## Unity Status

Unity remains part of the long-term architecture but is intentionally deferred in this merged v1. The current canonical runtime supports `stub` and `panorama` modes end-to-end.

## Docs

- [Docs Index](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/README.md)
- [Architecture](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)
- [Runtime Flow](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/runtime-flow.md)
- [Notebook Workspace](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/notebooks.md)
- [Memory Transparency](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/memory-transparency.md)
- [Validation Guide](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/validation.md)
- [Status](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/status.md)
- [Migration Map](https://github.com/ZSturman/Episodic-Memory-Agent/blob/main/docs/migration-map.md)
